supporting_websites.snippet: On June 26, 2026, the Indian stock market, including the Bombay Stock Exchange (BSE) and the National Stock Exchange (NSE), is closed in observance of Muharram. Trading activities are suspended for the day due to this religious holiday.

supporting_websites.title: Stocks slip as Apple price hikes fan tech jitters, oil slides
supporting_websites.snippet:
Global stock markets declined on Friday, primarily driven by concerns over inflation linked to Apple’s latest price hikes, which reflect increased costs for memory and storage chips. This move triggered broader unease about tech sector spending and its inflationary impact. Apple’s shares had dropped 6% on Thursday and remained under scrutiny. Adding to the market's cautious mood, reports emerged that OpenAI might delay its IPO to next year.

Meanwhile, oil prices fell over 3%, nearing four-month lows despite ongoing challenges reopening the Strait of Hormuz. Contributing to the downward pressure on oil was Saudi Aramco’s resumption of exports from Ras Tanura, likely increasing global supply. European stocks dropped about 1%, with tech falling 2%, while Wall Street futures pointed to additional declines. Asian tech stocks also suffered, with South Korea’s KOSPI plunging up to 9%, triggering a trading halt.

Currency markets saw the yen approach its weakest level in 40 years against the dollar, despite in-line U.S. inflation data and reduced expectations of a Federal Reserve rate hike. The U.S. economy grew faster in Q1 due to lower imports, but nearly flat consumer spending raised concerns for Q2. U.S. Treasury yields fell, and gold prices rose slightly by 0.5% to $4,046.

Global stock markets declined on Friday, primarily driven by concerns over inflation linked to Apple’s latest price hikes, which reflect increased costs for memory and storage chips. This move triggered broader unease about tech sector spending and its inflationary impact. Apple’s shares had dropped 6% on Thursday and remained under scrutiny. Adding to the market's cautious mood, reports emerged that OpenAI might delay its IPO to next year.

Meanwhile, oil prices fell over 3%, nearing four-month lows despite ongoing challenges reopening the Strait of Hormuz. Contributing to the downward pressure on oil was Saudi Aramco’s resumption of exports from Ras Tanura, likely increasing global supply. European stocks dropped about 1%, with tech falling 2%, while Wall Street futures pointed to additional declines. Asian tech stocks also suffered, with South Korea’s KOSPI plunging up to 9%, triggering a trading halt.

Currency markets saw the yen approach its weakest level in 40 years against the dollar, despite in-line U.S. inflation data and reduced expectations of a Federal Reserve rate hike. The U.S. economy grew faster in Q1 due to lower imports, but nearly flat consumer spending raised concerns for Q2. U.S. Treasury yields fell, and gold prices rose slightly by 0.5% to $4,046.

supporting_websites.title: Markets fall as tech worries rise; Brexit and Trump tariffs take bite out of UK food exports - business live
supporting_websites.snippet:
Global financial markets are under pressure as investor sentiment shifts due to falling tech stocks and geopolitical risks. The FTSE 100 and FTSE 250 indices in the UK fell, following similar losses across European and Asian markets. Key tech companies like Nvidia, Palantir, and Oracle are driving the decline, with growing fears that the AI boom may fuel inflation rather than spur economic growth.

In the UK, food and drink exports dropped to their lowest in a decade due to Brexit-related trade friction and US tariffs, with exports to the US falling 28%. This also resulted in a significant reduction in the UK’s export surplus with the US. At the same time, a heatwave has increased electricity demand, prompting energy supply warnings and requests for additional capacity in Britain.

Pubs and bars saw a surge in customers due to the heatwave, though London restaurants recorded a drop in activity. Meanwhile, Volkswagen plans to cut up to 100,000 jobs, and Brent crude prices eased after tensions in the Strait of Hormuz. Ofgem has favored 16 new energy storage projects to reduce reliance on fossil fuels, emphasizing the UK’s push for clean energy investments amid international conflict and climate challenges.

v.v: - Technology and semiconductor stocks remain volatile after recent AI-driven rallies. citeturn0news3

supporting_websites.title: Wall St Week Ahead Jobs data, rate bets in focus as US stocks close solid first half
supporting_websites.snippet:
U.S. markets are heading into the end of a strong first half of 2026, with the S&P 500 up over 7% year-to-date. However, June has been more volatile, especially for technology and semiconductor stocks. Investor focus is now turning to upcoming U.S. jobs data, due Thursday, which could influence expectations for Federal Reserve interest rate hikes. A strong report may increase fears of a rate hike, as the Fed remains focused on controlling inflation, which has surpassed 4% due to a spike in energy prices amid Middle East tensions.

Semiconductor stocks, which have led tech performance, remain a market focal point, though some investors are reassessing the sustainability of AI-related gains. Micron's strong earnings provided temporary support to the sector, but concerns linger about the impact of higher interest rates on tech and broader markets.

Economists expect moderate job growth in June, following months of solid gains. Fed funds futures now suggest increased odds of a rate hike by September, shifting sentiment from earlier in the year when rate cuts were anticipated. Meanwhile, investors are also watching corporate earnings, including Nike’s results next week, and geopolitical developments that could affect oil prices and inflation.
